data.title: Why The Endless Bitcoin, Ethereum Sideways Trend May Be A Good Sign
data.description:
Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) remain above a key weekly support zones, reinforcing confidence that the broader long-term uptrend remains intact.

Bitcoin’s Resilience Signals Long-Term Confidence

Crypto trader Jelle noted that despite weakness on lower timeframes and the daily chart, Bitcoin is still holding critical weekly support, preserving its long-term bullish structure.

Short-term price action may look shaky, but the higher-timeframe trend has not broken.

Macro conditions are also turning supportive. Quantitative tightening has ended and rates are moving lower, an environment that has historically favoured risk assets, particularly Bitcoin.

Jelle added that extended periods of weakness have been a recurring theme this cycle, often flushing out weak hands before the next leg higher. The current pullback fits that pattern rather than signalling a structural breakdown.

Are We Witnessing A Bullish Divergence?

Jelle shared that he already took substantial profits earlier in the bull run and now holds a smaller BTC "moonbag," roughly 15% of his original position.

Given the current setup, he sees no reason to sell further and is content waiting for the next move.

He also maintains a sizable Ethereum position, noting ETH's relative strength during BTC's consolidation. The expectation is for ETH to outperform once Bitcoin regains momentum, with profits taken gradually.

However, a sustained move below $2,500 would trigger a reassessment.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: 5 Tech Stocks Surging as Wall Street Money Floods East
data.description:
Investing in China is always fraught with political tensions from the ruling government. Alibaba and Tencent’s woes are just two recent examples. Still, if you have the stomach to deal with that type of risk, you might find Chinese stocks very appealing.

Chinese companies have made several impressive advances in the electric vehicle, AI, and renewable energy industries over the last few years, and the Chinese government continues to signal a commitment to stimulate the economy and capital markets.

Despite this accommodating policy, and their technological successes, Chinese stocks still trade at deep discounts to peers in other countries, and especially to those in the US.

But last year shows this may be changing. Stocks in several countries outside the US grew much faster than here, and investment flows away from the US are only increasing.

So today, we’re looking at five stocks leading the charge in the Chinese equity market, many of which are expanding their international footprint as the U.S. pulls back from international trade.

BYD Co. Ltd.

One of the biggest beneficiaries of the political pushback against Tesla's Elon Musk has been Chinese EV maker BYD Co. (OTCPK:BYDDY), which has become the world’s top electric vehicle seller over the last year. The company now has a $116 billion market cap and delivered more than 2.2 million vehicles in 2025, with earnings of over $108 billion in revenue (in USD). BYD is a vertically integrated company, meaning most components, such as batteries and electronics, are made under the same corporate umbrella. This business model gives BYD a wide moat and the ability to scale quickly to meet demand. With EV adoption rising each year, BYD is well-positioned to become the global leader in battery-powered vehicles.

Despite impressive growth, BYD's stock has struggled to break above the 50-day simple moving average (SMA) since falling into a bear market last year. But the decline appears to be stalling as the stock recently broke above the 50-day SMA resistance level with bullish momentum appearing on both the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator and the Relative Strength Index (RSI).

2 Stocks Primed to Soar As ‘Round Two’ of the AI Boom Starts

The first surge of AI stocks made early investors rich. Now, ‘Round Two' could be even bigger. But the next wave of winners could look nothing like the last. Discover the two stocks best positioned for this monumental shift. Get Both AI Tickers Here (For Free)

Alibaba Group Holdings Ltd.

Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) is the largest e-commerce company in China, with a market cap of over $400 billion and annual sales of nearly $140 billion as of last year. Jack Ma's infamous dispute with the ruling CCP appears to have subsided (but it’s a good reminder to always consider country risk when investing internationally), and the company's AliCloud business is one of its fastest-growing segments.

A shift toward higher margin services like cloud, logistics, and digital media has boosted the company's outlook, which is why the 18 analysts with BABA coverage tracked by Benzinga label the stock a consensus Buy with an average price target of $173, which is right around where the stock is today. However, the three most recent price targets from Jeffries, Citigroup, and JPMorgan average to $226, implying upside of more than 30%.

While the long-term has remained bullish, BABA shares spent most of the second half of 2025 battling the 50-day SMA in a rangebound trading pattern. But the bullish MACD cross coincided with a breakout above the 50-day SMA, and the stock is now testing the former resistance level. With the RSI and MACD trending up, the 50-day SMA is likely to switch from resistance to support as bullish momentum accumulates.

Baidu Inc.

The “Google of China” has been on an impressive run, and it may only be getting started. Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) is the country's largest search platform, owning more than 70% of the market share for internet searches. Given China's highly restrictive media environment, Baidu's search penetration is unlikely to be undone by competitors anytime soon.

However, the company's ERNIE AI chatbot model has investors thinking about Baidu's next steps as a tech giant. The ERNIE platform and the company's cloud business have shown rapid usage (and revenue) growth, and the stock has already received two price target bumps in 2026 from Jeffries and Freedom Capital Markets, respectively.

The $55 billion tech giant also trades at just 14 times earnings, and its stock appears poised to enter a new bull market in the new year. BIDU shares had been trading close to the 50-day SMA support level until the MACD lines crossed the histogram in late December, triggering a huge breakout and pushing the share price to levels not seen since late 2021. Baidu's next catalyst is its February 26 earnings report, with analysts expecting $4.36 billion in quarterly revenue.

XPeng Inc.

XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) is a smaller (but still growing) competitor to BYD, with an $18 billion market cap and several models of battery-powered cars and SUVs available. Unlike BYD, XPeng remains in the pre-profit stage of its business, but losses are narrowing, and the company delivered more than 116,000 vehicles in Q3 2025, representing year-over-year (YOY) growth of nearly 150%. The company's next earnings release is scheduled for March 17, and analysts are projecting record revenue of $3.32 billion. 

The XPEV chart is choppy, but it is showing signs of a breakout after a long period of consolidation. The share price has been bouncing between the 50-day and 200-day SMAs as the two lines converge. The 200-day SMA dipping under the 50-day SMA would be a bearish signal, but the MACD shows that bullish momentum is starting to grow, and the next sharp move could be to the upside.

VNET Group Inc.

VNet (NASDAQ:VNET) is a data center and cloud infrastructure company with a $2.8 billion market cap at the heart of China's AI boom. VNet directly benefits from AI data center buildouts, as its clients are large cloud and enterprise companies, and its carrier-neutral status allows it to serve multiple providers simultaneously. China's data laws also create strong barriers to entry in this space, and VNet benefits from being a trusted incumbent with a large moat. In its Q3 2025 report, VNet raised full-year revenue guidance after its wholesale revenue surged 82.7% YOY.

VNET shares are attempting their own breakout after finally ripping above the 50-day SMA after several months of bouncing between the 50-day and 200-day marks. The stock appears to be at a crossroads now, however, with the price rechallenging the 50-day SMA while the MACD and signal lines converge. The RSI is trending upward, which offers some optimism for the stock’s direction, but the next few sessions will be crucial for determining VNET's short-term trajectory (the long-term trend remains bullish, though).

data.title: GameStop Stock Soars As Burry Reveals Fresh Stake, Social Media Hype Returns
data.description:
GameStop Corp (NYSE:GME) shares are extending recent strength Monday afternoon after famed investor Michael Burry disclosed he has been buying shares of the video game retailer, adding fresh fuel to a rally already driven by CEO Ryan Cohen's insider purchases.

  • GameStop stock is showing exceptional strength. Why is GME stock surging?

GameStop Stock Jumps On New Michael Burry Buy

In a new Substack post shared on X, Burry wrote, "I own GME. I have been buying recently.” The famed investor, known for shorting the U.S. housing market before 2008, believes he is buying near one-times tangible book value, highlighting Cohen's role in deploying the company's capital over the long term. His comments quickly circulated across social media platforms, sparking a sharp jump in shares of GameStop on Monday.

Shares jumped intraday to around $25, up roughly 8% on the session. Monday's move builds on gains sparked by disclosures that Cohen bought one million GameStop shares in the open market last week at prices just above $21, lifting his stake to about 42.1 million shares, or 9.3% of shares outstanding.

The company also recently approved a new compensation plan that could grant Cohen options on roughly 171.5 million additional shares if GameStop achieves ambitious long-term performance targets, including a potential $100 billion market capitalization and $10 billion in cumulative EBITDA.

Why Michael Burry's Return To GME Moves The Stock

Burry's renewed interest matters because he was one of the earliest high-profile investors to champion GameStop's turnaround. Through his firm Scion Asset Management, he disclosed a sizable GME stake in 2019 and pushed the company to buy back stock, cut debt and streamline operations, arguments that helped legitimize the contrarian bull case well before the 2021 meme-stock explosion.

Although Burry exited prior to the 2021 short squeeze, his original thesis that the balance sheet and loyal customer base were undervalued became a reference point for many retail traders. Seeing him return now, at what he frames as close to book value and with Cohen firmly in charge, reinforces the idea that a seasoned value investor still sees asymmetric upside.

Traders across social media are taking notice. GameStop was trending over the weekend on socials as Domo Capital Management’s Justin Dopierala announced that he was invested in GameStop stock again.

“We’re back in $GME! And I don’t think we’ll be the only one back,” Domo Capital Management said in a series of X posts.

GameStop's Business Model

GameStop is a U.S. multichannel video game, consumer electronics, and services retailer. The company operates across Europe, Canada, Australia, and the United States, primarily selling new and second-hand video game hardware, software, and accessories through its GameStop, EB Games, and Micromania stores, as well as international e-commerce sites.

Earnings Report: A Turning Point Ahead?

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Mar. 24.

  • EPS Estimate: 31 cents (Up from 30 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.47 billion (Up from $1.28 billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 26.1x (Indicates premium valuation)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for GameStop, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 69.5 — Trading at a premium valuation relative to peers.
  • Growth: 96.21 — Indicates significant growth potential.
  • Momentum: 17.12 — Suggests the stock is underperforming in terms of price momentum.

The Verdict: GameStop's Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the strong growth score indicates potential, the weak momentum score suggests caution for investors.

GME Shares Surge Monday Afternoon

GME Price Action: GameStop shares were up 7.39% at $24.69 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Cybersecurity Meets Monthly Income: Amplify's New ETF Targets 15%+ Yield
data.description:
Amplify ETFs has launched a new options-based cybersecurity ETF to meet the needs of investors wanting monthly income from a sector more associated with growth than income generation.

• What’s driving HAKY shares up?

The new fund, referred to as the Amplify HACK Cybersecurity Covered Call ETF (NYSE:HAKY), will be comprised of shares of the Amplify Cybersecurity ETF (NYSE:HACK) and call options on the ETF, generating income from the premiums of the call options. The ETF seeks a 15% or more annualized option premium income and a monthly distribution.

This is in contrast to traditional income funds based on dividends, bonds, etc., which HAKY’s strategy seeks to benefit from with its covered call strategy, which performs better in volatile conditions, sacrificing some growth in the event of a strong equity market run. It claims to offer an income stream with partial upside participation in the potential gains of cybersecurity equities.

The move comes as the cybersecurity expenditure continues to grow, driven by mounting digital risks, stricter regulatory demands and the adoption of artificial intelligence solutions within organizations.

Projected figures by Cybersecurity Ventures indicate the cybersecurity industry will be valued at over $1 trillion by 2031, a clear indicator of the growing investments in the sector over the years.

HAKY is the latest addition to Amplify's suite of YieldSmart ETFs, which are options-based ETFs that integrate yield with exposure to publicly traded equities. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.65%.

Though such covered call strategies may prove useful in providing support to returns during volatile market conditions, when equities soar, these strategies might not perform as well, making HAKY more appropriate for an income-oriented rather than an appreciation-oriented investor.

data.title: Insights Ahead: World Acceptance's Quarterly Earnings
data.description:
World Acceptance (NASDAQ:WRLD) will release its quarterly earnings report on Tuesday, 2026-01-27. Here's a brief overview for investors ahead of the announcement.

Analysts anticipate World Acceptance to report an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.79.

World Acceptance bulls will hope to hear the company announce they've not only beaten that estimate, but also to provide positive guidance, or forecasted growth, for the next quarter.

New investors should note that it is sometimes not an earnings beat or miss that most affects the price of a stock, but the guidance (or forecast).

Past Earnings Performance

Last quarter the company missed EPS by $1.79, which was followed by a 8.9% drop in the share price the next day.

Here's a look at World Acceptance's past performance and the resulting price change:

Performance of World Acceptance Shares

Shares of World Acceptance were trading at $139.08 as of January 23. Over the last 52-week period, shares are down 6.19%. Given that these returns are generally negative, long-term shareholders are likely unhappy going into this earnings release.

To track all earnings releases for World Acceptance visit their earnings calendar on our site.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.
